notes: Kiro CLI maintains a single-session task list via the built-in todo_list tool (create with N distinct tasks, then complete each by 0-indexed number; gated by the experimental chat.enableTodoList setting). Driver asks kiro to track three tasks and walk each to completed in one session. The lifecycle arc (ready→working→ready) and SubagentCount=0 are observable and asserted as phases. KNOWN FAILING: the load-bearing tasks-content observable — three items in the session API tasks field with matching subjects + final status — is NOT produced because the kiro parser routes every toolUse (including todo_list) through the generic opaque ToolUse{ID,Name} path and emits no TaskDelta/TaskSnapshot (the local JSONL trace exists and carries the todo_list call + its task arguments, so this is a parser bug, not an architectural incapability, mirroring codex update_plan). The spec asserts the CORRECT behavior; record files the issue and records known_failing. The kiro JSONL carries no model/token/cost (sidecar-only, not read by the v1 parser), so observations are model=unknown, cost_nonzero=false, tokens_nonzero=false.

text: Kiro session appears in ready (first phase anchored UNPINNED to start so a transient proc-<PID> presession can't steal birth)
User prompt drives the session ready → working (no delay bound — kiro turn-start latency is unbounded)

invariants: no state_transition to waiting for session
no state_transition to ready for session
text: Session stays working across the todo_list create + complete calls — the tool auto-executes under --trust-all-tools (no permission gate), so no waiting fires and no premature ready dip; no child sessions spawn (SubagentCount stays 0)
The final text-only AssistantMessage drives working → ready at turn end; final session state is ready; no child sessions were spawned
